description: Joined Myceli.ai OÜ — the company operating Pollinations.ai — as a Developer Relations Intern. Driving developer advocacy, contributor onboarding, Discord and issue moderation, app-submission review, and growing the contributor and developer community across the open-source generative-AI platform.

Maintainer at Pollinations.ai — building the contributor pipeline infrastructure: Pollen-Quest payouts, project-manager bot, app-submission workflows, model curation, AI-assisted PR review, and the Polli CLI. Also contributed backend and community-app work across the platform.
Invited as a GitHub Maintainer Advisory member, providing insights and guidance to enhance the mentorship experience for aspiring developers and open-source contributors.
Positioned as the GDG Campus Lead at JIS University, organizing events and workshops to promote Google technologies and foster a strong developer community.

I was a web dev associate for the Google Developers Group Club of JIS University, where I had conducted 3 engaging tech-talk sessions, with satisfactory participation, with the privilege of collaborating with other GDG clubs and speakers in my tenure.
Positioned as an OSCI Mentor, guiding and supporting students in their open-source contributions and helping them navigate the complexities of collaborative software development.
Positioned as the GSSoC Mentor, guiding and supporting students in their open-source contributions and helping them navigate the complexities of collaborative software development.
I used to develop mods for Minecraft on Modrinth, my overall downloads are 1K+ and the projects are still listed on the Modrinth page. It was a fun part-time involvement while I was learning Java.
